name: Hawaiian Upside-Down Cake
recipeIngredient: Granulated Sugar, C and H 3/4 cup
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a 10-inch oven-proof skillet.
2. Sprinkle brown sugar evenly over the melted butter, then add pineapple, coconut, and cherries. Set aside.
3. Beat eggs in a mixing bowl until light and thick (about 3 to 5 minutes).
4. Gradually beat in granulated sugar.
5. Add reserved pineapple juice and vanilla.
6. Stir in dry ingredients until smooth.
7. Pour the batter over the topping in the skillet.
8. Bake at 350°F for 40 to 45 minutes.
9. Cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes.
10. Invert onto serving plate.
Makes one 10-inch cake, serving 8.
